sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CBOE Global (CBOE) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: CBOE Global (CBOE) is expected to report a significant year-over-year increase in earnings and revenues for the quarter ending December 2025. With a Zacks Rank #1 and a positive Earnings ESP of +1.55%, the company is predicted to beat the consensus EPS estimate. Investors are advised to consider other factors beyond earnings beats or misses, although a positive Earnings ESP combined with a strong Zacks Rank increases the odds of success.

feed.title: Buy, Sell or Hold QCOM Stock? Key Tips Ahead of Q1 Earnings Release
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) is set to report its Q1 fiscal 2026 earnings on Feb. 4, with estimates for revenues and earnings per share. Despite new AI-focused product launches likely boosting revenue, the company faces challenges from an antitrust probe in China and stiff competition. The stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) given declining earnings estimates and market uncertainties.

feed.title: BNY dismissed from Epstein case, but BofA must face some claims
feed.summary: A federal judge dismissed all Jeffrey Epstein-related claims against BNY, affirming its non-involvement in Epstein's crimes. However, some claims against Bank of America were allowed to proceed, including accusations that it was a "knowing beneficiary" in a sex trafficking venture and obstructed federal enforcement of trafficking laws. Bank of America expressed satisfaction that most claims were dismissed and looks forward to a full review of the facts for the remaining claims.

feed.title: Assurant, Inc. (NYSE:AIZ) to Issue Quarterly Dividend of $0.88
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. (NYSE:AIZ) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.88 per share, payable on March 30th to shareholders of record as of February 17th. This marks the 21st consecutive year the company has raised its dividend, which is well-covered by earnings with a payout ratio of 18.1%. Analysts project consistent earnings coverage for next year's expected dividend.

feed.title: Otis Worldwide Corporation Announces Quarterly Dividend of $0.42 (NYSE:OTIS)
feed.summary: Otis Worldwide Corporation (NYSE:OTIS) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.42 per share, payable on March 13th to shareholders of record by February 13th, representing an annualized dividend of $1.68 and a yield of 2.0%. The dividend is well-covered by earnings, with a payout ratio of 37.4% and an expected future payout ratio of 38.0%. The company recently reported Q4 EPS of $1.03, meeting estimates, and revenue of $3.80 billion, slightly below consensus, with shares trading down about 1.2%.

feed.title: Jacobs Solutions Inc. (NYSE:J) to Issue Dividend Increase - $0.36 Per Share
feed.summary: Jacobs Solutions Inc. (NYSE:J) has announced a dividend increase to $0.36 per share, up 12.5% from the previous $0.32, payable on March 20th to shareholders of record on February 20th. This brings its annualized dividend to $1.28, representing a 1.1% yield. The company demonstrates strong dividend coverage with a payout ratio of 18.6% and has consistently raised its dividend for the past four years, with analysts projecting continued strong earnings.

feed.title: J&J wins EU backing for Akeega label expansion (JNJ:NYSE)
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) has received a positive recommendation from the European Medicines Agency (EMA)'s expert panel for an expanded indication for its prostate cancer drug, Akeega. Akeega is co-marketed with GSK (GSK). This endorsement signifies a potential label expansion for the therapy within the EU.

feed.title: Google stock price today: GOOG steadies as Alphabet nears earnings amid legal headlines
feed.summary: Alphabet's Class C shares (GOOG) traded sideways as the company approaches its quarterly earnings report next week, with investors closely watching for updates on AI spending and cloud growth. The stock's performance is further complicated by recent legal developments, including a $135 million class action settlement and the conviction of a former engineer for stealing AI trade secrets. The market is assessing whether upcoming megacap earnings will sustain the AI rally or reveal potential overspending issues.

feed.title: The Charles Schwab Corporation (NYSE:SCHW) Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.32
feed.summary: The Charles Schwab Corporation announced a quarterly dividend of $0.32 per share, representing an 18.5% increase from its previous quarter's payout. The dividend is payable on February 27th to shareholders of record on February 13th, with an ex-dividend date of February 13th. The company's management views the dividend as sustainable, with a payout ratio near 21% and expected future payout of approximately 20.7%.

feed.title: Ace Hardware, Menards lead key trend Home Depot and Lowe’s missed
feed.summary: Ace Hardware and Menards are outperforming big-box retailers like Home Depot and Lowe's in consumer satisfaction, particularly due to superior customer service and helpful staff, according to a recent Market Force study. Despite Home Depot leading in purchase share, customer loyalty metrics indicate that service quality is a driving factor for repeat business. This trend highlights a shift in the home improvement retail landscape where customer experience is becoming a critical battleground against larger competitors, with Menards also excelling through its store layout and price competitiveness.

feed.title: MarketAxess (MKTX) Expected to Beat Earnings Estimates: Can the Stock Move Higher?
feed.summary: MarketAxess (MKTX) is expected to report a year-over-year decline in earnings but higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. With a positive Earnings ESP of +0.05% and a Zacks Rank #3, the company is likely to beat the consensus EPS estimate of $1.66 per share. Investors should consider their strong past performance of consistently beating EPS estimates.

feed.title: Newport’s new Kroger opens in West end
feed.summary: Newport's new Kroger store, located at 20 West 6th St., has officially opened after a $14 million investment. The store includes a pharmacy and gas station, with a full-service wine and spirits shop planned to open nearby later this year. To celebrate, a customer appreciation day is scheduled for January 31, and gas customers will receive 20 cents off per gallon until February 28.

feed.title: Costco expands Instacart delivery to Europe
feed.summary: Costco is launching same-day delivery services in France and Spain through a partnership with Instacart, expanding their existing collaboration in North America. This initiative will allow Costco members in these European countries to order groceries online for same-day delivery, utilizing Instacart's technology and fulfillment services. The expansion aims to enhance convenience and accessibility for Costco members while maintaining in-store pricing.

feed.title: Restaurant Group With 2,200 Locations Files for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
feed.summary: FAT Brands Inc. and Twin Hospitality Group Inc., parent companies of prominent restaurant chains like Twin Peaks and Smokey Bones, have filed for voluntary Chapter 11 bankruptcy. This move comes after Investor 352 Fund sued FAT Brands for $109 million and amid reports of the company owing $1.26 billion to debtors. Despite the bankruptcy, both Twin Peaks and Smokey Bones plan to remain operational, with the companies aiming to strengthen their financial structures and continue growth.

feed.title: The Mag 7 are no longer trading as a unified group – T. Rowe Price’s Tony Wang (AAPL:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Tony Wang of T. Rowe Price observes that the "Magnificent Seven" tech companies are no longer trading as a unified group due to differing strategies and valuations. While Meta saw a stock surge stemming from AI investments, Microsoft's AI spending did not clearly translate to revenue upside. Large-cap tech companies are best positioned in the evolving AI cycle due to their scale.

feed.title: Early & Accessible Investing
feed.summary: Black Titan Corp (BTTC) is exploring strategies to leverage the growing trend of fractionalization in investments, aiming to redefine asset management and unlock capital efficiency. The company, a Cayman Islands-incorporated technology and human capital management solutions group, is expanding its platform to support data-driven digital asset initiatives. This move is in response to technology dismantling traditional investment barriers and democratizing the investment landscape.

feed.title: G2 To Buy Capterra, Software Advice, And GetApp From Gartner
feed.summary: G2 has announced its agreement to acquire Capterra, Software Advice, and GetApp from Gartner, aiming to consolidate major B2B software review platforms. This merger is intended to enhance data foundations for software discovery, evaluation, and purchasing, leveraging AI-driven experiences. The combined platforms are expected to feature around 6 million verified customer reviews and reach over 200 million annual software buyers globally.

feed.title: Genesco CFO Sandra Harris Will Exit in March
feed.summary: Genesco's CFO, Sandra Harris, will step down on March 6 after serving since October 2024, to pursue other opportunities. CEO Mimi Vaughn, who was previously CFO from 2015 to 2019, will assume the interim CFO role while the company searches for a permanent replacement. Harris will assist with the transition and participate in the Q4 and fiscal year 2026 earnings conference call.

feed.title: Apollo's Strong Q3 Results Propel Price Forecast Upgrade — Analyst Highlights Asset Growth And Future Potential
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management reported strong third-quarter results, with adjusted EPS of $1.85 and revenue of $7.77 billion, surpassing analyst estimates. The company now manages $733 billion in assets and saw significant inflows and capital deployment over the past year. JPMorgan analyst Kenneth B. Worthington raised Apollo's price target to $161, citing the company's asset growth and confidence in its ambitious 2029 financial targets.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks Completes Chronosphere Acquisition
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has acquired Chronosphere, a cloud-scale observability platform, to enhance its ability to provide deep visibility and security across cloud-native environments and AI systems. This acquisition aims to address the challenge of securing massive volumes of real-time data in the AI era by integrating Chronosphere's platform with Cortex AgentiX. The combined offering will provide essential operational and security telemetry for autonomous remediation and help reduce data-related "data tax" for enterprises.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern stock beats Q4 estimates, raises 2026 productivity target
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) reported strong fourth-quarter results, with adjusted EPS of $3.22 significantly exceeding estimates and diluted EPS of $12.75 for the last year. The company's performance was bolstered by an $85 million expense reduction from a land sale and a $50 million increase in net income due to a state tax resolution. Looking ahead, Norfolk Southern has raised its 2026 productivity savings target to $150 million, while also addressing competitive challenges related to a merger announcement and continuing to provide information to the Surface Transportation Board.

feed.title: PNC Foundation Contributes $2M for New Snow Removal Equipment to City of Pittsburgh
feed.summary: The PNC Foundation has granted $2 million to the City of Pittsburgh for new snow removal equipment following a recent record-setting snowstorm. This donation will enable the Department of Public Works to purchase 15 new vehicles, bringing their total new equipment to over 50 pieces. The initiative aims to enhance the city's preparedness for severe winter weather and strengthen community resilience.

feed.title: Stock Movers: Apple, American Express Decker Rises
feed.summary: This article discusses the performance of Apple, Decker, and American Express stocks. Apple shares saw a slight fall despite upbeat earnings and a strong forecast, while Decker's stock rose due to increased earnings and sales forecasts. American Express shares dropped due to higher-than-expected expenses from its Platinum card refresh.

feed.title: Home Depot (Atlanta) WARN Act Investigation
feed.summary: Strauss Borrelli PLLC is investigating Home Depot U.S.A, Inc. regarding a potential mass layoff of 797 employees in Atlanta, Georgia, announced on January 28, 2026. The law firm believes Home Depot may have violated the federal WARN Act by failing to provide the required 60 days' notice before the layoffs, potentially entitling affected employees to 60 days of severance pay and benefits. The WARN Act mandates that employers with 100 or more employees give 60 days' notice for significant layoffs or plant closings to allow workers time to prepare.

feed.title: MarineMax stock falls 9% despite sales growth as Benchmark maintains Buy rating
feed.summary: MarineMax (NYSE:HZO) shares dropped nearly 9% despite reporting stronger-than-expected revenue and a 10% increase in comparable sales. The decline followed the company's mixed quarterly earnings, which missed analyst expectations for adjusted EBITDA and earnings per share, though Benchmark maintained its Buy rating and $35.00 price target. Benchmark viewed the company's unchanged full-year guidance positively, citing the company is overcoming past hurricane impacts and managing seasonal margin pressures.

feed.title: Federal Signal to outline Q4 earnings in Feb. 25 investor call
feed.summary: Federal Signal (NYSE: FSS) will release its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings before the market opens on Wednesday, February 25, 2026. The company will host an investor conference call and webcast at 10:00 a.m. Eastern Time on the same day, featuring President and CEO Jennifer L. Sherman and SVP and CFO Ian A. Hudson. Investors can access the webcast and replay details on the company's website.

feed.title: Genesco on hunt for new CFO
feed.summary: Genesco, parent company of Journeys, announced that its CFO, Cassandra “Sandra” Harris, will step down on March 6 to pursue other opportunities. CEO Mimi E. Vaughn will serve as interim CFO while the company searches for a permanent replacement. Genesco is seeking a strategic leader to continue driving shareholder value.

feed.title: BTIG Research Adjusts Xcel Energy PT to $94 From $96, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: BTIG Research has adjusted its price target for Xcel Energy (XEL) to $94, down from $96, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This update comes amidst several other analyst adjustments for Xcel Energy in recent weeks, with various firms providing revised price targets and maintaining or changing their ratings. The article also provides a company profile for Xcel Energy, detailing its business segments, employee count, and upcoming earnings release.

feed.title: Should Orla Mining’s 2026 Production Step-Up and Guidance Shift Require Action From Orla Mining (TSX:OLA) Investors?
feed.summary: Orla Mining Ltd. reported strong gold production for Q4 2025 and full-year 2025, and issued 2026 guidance indicating a significant production step-up, particularly in the second half. This shift requires investors to focus on the company's operational execution and cost control, especially given its strong share price performance and rich earnings multiple. While meeting guidance could support the investment thesis, unmet targets or cost surprises could lead to a market reassessment.

feed.title: How Investors May Respond To Simon Property Group (SPG) Ahead Of 2025 Results And Premium Mall Focus
feed.summary: Ahead of its Q4 and full-year 2025 results, Simon Property Group (SPG) is being highlighted by analysts for its robust premium mall portfolio, high occupancy, and strong liquidity. The company's focus on mixed-use redevelopment and omnichannel retail initiatives is seen as strengthening its market position, though investors should be mindful of valuation, leverage, and the high expectations set for upcoming earnings. Multiple fair value estimates suggest a range of opinions on SPG's prospects.

feed.title: What Are Wall Street Analysts' Target Price for Ingersoll Rand Stock?
feed.summary: Ingersoll Rand Inc. (IR) has shown mixed stock performance, underperforming the broader market over the past year but surging in 2026. Analysts currently rate IR as a "Moderate Buy" with a mean price target of $90.07, suggesting a small upside from current market prices, although a Street-high target indicates a potential rally of up to 12.5%. The company's recent acquisition of Scinomix is seen as a strategic move to strengthen its life sciences and automation footprint.

feed.title: Is GE Vernova Stock a Buy Now?
feed.summary: GE Vernova, spun off from General Electric in April 2024, has seen its stock price surge over 400% due to its strong position in the electricity industry, particularly with gas and wind turbines and a large backlog expected to grow to $200 billion by 2028. Despite its robust business fundamentals and future prospects, the stock appears significantly overvalued with a P/E ratio of 108 and a P/B ratio of nearly 21, making it more expensive than the average industrial or even technology stock. This high valuation suggests that investors may be pricing in perfection, making it a potentially risky investment for both value and growth investors at its current price.

feed.title: Brokers Suggest Investing in Palo Alto (PANW): Read This Before Placing a Bet
feed.summary: This article evaluates brokerage recommendations for Palo Alto Networks (PANW), which currently holds an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.59, indicating a "Buy" to "Strong Buy." However, it cautions investors against relying solely on these recommendations due to a potential positive bias from brokerage firms. Instead, it suggests using the Zacks Rank, a quantitative model based on earnings estimate revisions, as a more reliable indicator for near-term stock performance.

feed.title: Duke Energy urges customers to prepare for snow, high winds and bitter cold across the Carolinas
feed.summary: Duke Energy is urging customers in the Carolinas to prepare for a winter storm bringing snow, high winds, and bitter cold, which could lead to power outages, especially in areas with weakened trees. The company advises customers to have emergency kits ready, download their app, and know how to report outages, while assuring that crews will work to restore power safely and efficiently despite potential delays due to hazardous conditions.

feed.title: Can Deckers Outdoor sustain momentum? Analysts split on growth potential (DECK:NYSE)
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor (DECK) stock jumped 13% after reporting record revenue and profits and an optimistic FY26 outlook, reaching a four-month high. However, analysts are split on the company's future growth, with concerns about UGG's sustained popularity and HOKA's competition in the running segment, while bulls highlight brand diversification and strong financials.

feed.title: Genpact Achieves CDP 'A' Rating for Climate Action
feed.summary: Genpact (NYSE: G) has been awarded an 'A' rating by CDP for Climate Action, placing it among global leaders in environmental transparency and climate performance. The company’s recognition highlights its strong commitment to sustainability, rigorous climate reporting, and proactive climate risk management, with sustainability embedded into its core business strategy. Genpact was among only 4% of nearly 20,000 companies to achieve an 'A' score in the latest CDP assessment cycle.

feed.title: Ecopetrol, Frontera Energy to add 50 MW of solar for Colombian oilfields
feed.summary: Ecopetrol and Frontera Energy are partnering to develop 50 MW of solar power capacity for their oilfields in Colombia. This initiative aims to integrate renewable energy into their operations, marking a step towards decarbonization in the Colombian energy sector. The project aligns with broader regional trends of increasing solar energy adoption.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical Q4 2025 slides: Revenue falls 12% as cost-cutting offsets market weakness
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical reported a 12% Q4 2025 revenue decline to $1.973 billion and adjusted EPS of $0.75, with full-year revenue down 7% to $8.752 billion. Despite market weaknesses across all segments, the company's shares rose in premarket trading due to successful cost-cutting initiatives that exceeded goals and a continued focus on its Circular Economy platform. Eastman expects similar operating cash flow in 2026 and aims for further cost reductions to navigate economic headwinds.

feed.title: Deckers Outdoor (NYSE:DECK) Shares Gap Up on Earnings Beat
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor (NYSE:DECK) stock gapped up by approximately 15% after reporting better-than-expected Q3 earnings and revenue, and raising its full-year 2026 guidance. While analysts reacted with some upgrades and raised price targets, the consensus rating remains a "Hold" due to mixed sentiment. A key risk identified is around $110 million in FY26 tariff costs, which could impact margins despite some pass-through to consumers.

feed.title: FDA Decision on Eli Lilly's Weight Loss Pill Delayed to Q2. Is This Bad News for the Stock?
feed.summary: The FDA has slightly delayed its target action date for Eli Lilly's weight loss pill, orforglipron, to April 10, pushing it into Q2. This minor delay, coupled with rival Novo Nordisk's already approved GLP-1 pill, caused a slight dip in Eli Lilly's stock. However, an analyst suggests this isn't a significant concern, as market dominance is more about effectiveness and accessibility than being first-to-market.

feed.title: Unity stock falls alongside Take-Two, Roblox after Google’s Project Genie launch
feed.summary: Unity Software, Take-Two Interactive Software, and Roblox Corporation stocks fell after Google announced Project Genie, an AI-powered world creation tool. Investors are concerned about potential competition from Google's new prototype, particularly for established game development platforms and user-generated content platforms. Google's Project Genie allows users to create, explore, and remix interactive virtual worlds through text prompts and images, signaling Google's deeper push into immersive content creation.

feed.title: Cavco Industries (NASDAQ:CVCO) Shares Gap Down Following Weak Earnings
feed.summary: Cavco Industries (NASDAQ:CVCO) saw its shares gap down by about 14.5% after reporting disappointing earnings, missing both EPS and revenue estimates. The construction company reported $5.58 EPS against an expected $6.00 and revenue of $580.99 million compared to estimates of $593.37 million. Despite the weak quarter, analysts have mixed ratings, with a MarketBeat consensus of "Moderate Buy" and institutional investors owning a significant portion of the stock.

feed.title: Invesco Mortgage Q4 2025 slides: book value growth and dividend hike despite earnings dip
feed.summary: Invesco Mortgage Capital Inc. (IVR) reported mixed Q4 2025 results, with earnings per share slightly missing analyst expectations but seeing an increase in book value and a raised quarterly dividend. The company's revenue significantly surpassed forecasts, and its strategic focus on Agency residential mortgage-backed securities contributed to an 8.0% economic return. Despite potential market risks, Invesco Mortgage expresses optimism for the Agency mortgage sector due to low volatility and a steeper yield curve, aiming to continue delivering attractive shareholder returns.

feed.title: Franklin Resources Q1 profit exceeds consensus on net inflows (BEN:NYSE)
feed.summary: Franklin Resources (BEN) reported Q1 2026 earnings and revenue that surpassed analyst expectations, driven by a return to quarterly long-term net inflows. The asset management giant recorded an adjusted EPS of $0.70, exceeding the average analyst estimate of $0.55. This performance marks an increase from the previous quarter's $0.67 and $0.59 from the same period last year.

feed.title: ‘All Boats Are Rising’ in Data and Memory, Says Western Digital Finance Chief. Just Look at the Stocks.
feed.summary: Western Digital's recent strong earnings report has confirmed the growing excitement around the data and memory sector. The finance chief attributes this trend to increasing demand from hyperscalers, suggesting a broad upturn for companies in the industry. The article highlights Western Digital's long-term agreements for hard drive sales as a key factor in this positive outlook.

feed.title: Daiwa Capital Markets Issues Positive Forecast for The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) Stock Price
feed.summary: Daiwa Capital Markets has increased its price target for The Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) to $940 from $927, maintaining a "neutral" rating. This adjustment follows Goldman Sachs' strong Q4 performance, which beat analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue, alongside an increase in its quarterly dividend to $4.50. However, significant insider selling, totaling approximately $83.7 million last quarter, could temper investor enthusiasm despite the positive financial results and analyst upgrades.

feed.title: LKQ Corporation seeks inside sales coordinators for key locations
feed.summary: LKQ Corporation is actively recruiting Inside Sales Coordinators for key locations across Ohio, Florida, Tennessee, and Massachusetts. The company emphasizes competitive benefits and encourages interested candidates to apply through its website. This hiring initiative aligns with LKQ's strategic focus on team dedication and talent acquisition for sustained success, as highlighted in its recent 2025 achievements celebration.

feed.title: Five Companies That Came To Win This Week
feed.summary: This article highlights five companies that made significant strategic moves in the past week. Palo Alto Networks completed a major acquisition to boost its AI observability capabilities, while Cisco Systems officially launched its new 360 Partner Program and IBM updated its Partner Plus program with new incentives. Microsoft entered the AI processor market with its Maia 200 chip, and cloud security startup Upwind secured $250 million in funding, achieving a $1.5 billion valuation.

feed.title: Hanover Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ:HNVR) to Issue $0.10 Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Hanover Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ:HNVR) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.10 per share, payable on February 26th to shareholders of record on February 12th. This represents an annual dividend of $0.40 and a yield of approximately 1.7%, with a low payout ratio indicating the dividend is well-covered by earnings. Despite missing recent quarterly earnings expectations with $0.00 EPS versus a $0.51 consensus, the company's shares traded at $23.41, and analysts expect future earnings to continue covering the dividend.

feed.title: BNP Paribas Exane Issues Pessimistic Forecast for General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) Stock Price
feed.summary: BNP Paribas Exane has cut its price target for General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) from $420 to $410, though it maintained an "outperform" rating, suggesting a 17.1% upside. This adjustment comes despite General Dynamics beating Q4 earnings and revenue estimates, due to the company issuing a lower-than-expected FY-2026 EPS guidance of $16.10–$16.20 against the street's $17.26 estimate. The stock's performance has been pressured by this forecast, but analyst coverage mostly remains positive, with a consensus of "Moderate Buy."
